checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text present in the source document. The headings, legend items, and source information all correspond directly to text found in the map's legend and title blocks. No invented text or fabricated sections were found.
The source document does not contain specific contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or URLs. The output also does not contain any such information, ensuring no fabrications were made.

The source document consists of a single page containing a title, date, map, legend, and sources. All these discrete elements are represented in the output JSON. The map itself and the logo are included as image elements.
The source document does not contain any tables. The legend, while structured, is correctly represented as lists in the JSON.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All relevant text content from the title, date, legend, and sources has been captured. While the individual county names on the map are not listed as text, they are integral parts of the map image itself, which is provided as an image element. In the context of a map document, the omission of graphical labels from the primary text flow while providing the image is not a defect.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text captured in the headings and lists exactly matches the wording, dates, and punctuation found in the source document.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source image and the generated JSON output. Each text element from the source, including the main headings, the legend text, and the sources block, appears only once in the output. No paragraphs or list items are repeated.

The output correctly identifies the main document title as an H1 ('NC Parcels Project Status by County') and the date as an H2 ('January 8, 2025'). This follows a logical descending hierarchy suitable for the document's structure.
The source document contains no tables; it consists of text, a map (image), and a legend (list). The output correctly does not include any table elements.

The reading order in the JSON follows a logical top-to-bottom path: Title -> Date -> Legend -> Sources -> Visual elements (Map and Logo). This reflects the intended information flow of the original document.

The document's legend, which is visually a list of categories with corresponding colors, has been appropriately converted into unordered lists. The text within the legend ('Parcel Updates:', 'Most recent Quarter', and the specific date ranges) is preserved and correctly sequenced.
There are no links present in the source document, and none have been fabricated or injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document has been significantly altered. In the source PDF, the map is the central focus, positioned immediately below the header with the legend nested within its boundaries. In the HTML output, the map placeholder has been moved to the bottom of the document, and the legend has been extracted and converted into a vertical list that precedes the map and sources. This rearrangement disrupts the visual flow and relationship between the map and its key. Additionally, there is extraneous text at the top ('Map Imagery Logo Imagery F810') that is not present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: The map is the central visual element of the page, with the legend appearing within the map's area on the left.
checks.b_evidence_output: The map placeholder is moved to the bottom of the document, appearing after the 'Sources' section. The legend is rendered as a standalone list at the top of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; it is an infographic map. Therefore, this check is not applicable and passes.

The primary meaningful content of the document—the map of North Carolina and the NCGICC logo—are missing from the rendered HTML, showing only broken image icons and alt text. Furthermore, the color swatches in the 'Parcel Updates' legend, which are critical for interpreting the map's data, are completely absent from the HTML list rendering. This results in a complete loss of the document's visual data representation.
checks.b_evidence_source: Infographic map of North Carolina counties and NCGICC logo in bottom right. Legend contains colored swatches representing different quarters.
checks.b_evidence_output: Broken image icons with alt text ('A map of North Carolina counties...', 'NCGICC logo'). Legend is a plain text list without color swatches.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication. The titles and section headings appear once. While there is extra text at the top ('Map Imagery Logo Imagery'), it is not a duplication of existing content blocks.
